hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions through aromatic substitutions at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis should be revised to emphasize the importance of disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to achieve SSTR2 selectivity. Strong ddG alone is insufficient if off-target binding remains high.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 can help break off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

Introducing diverse mutations at non-pharmacophore positions can disrupt conserved pocket interactions in off-target SSTRs, especially SSTR3/SSTR5, while maintaining the pharmacophore for SSTR2 binding.
